relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Earthly masters are placed under the heavenly Master and called to justice and fairness.
Paul asks for an open door to speak Messiah’s mystery.
The mystery must be made manifest in the right speech.
Wisdom walks toward outsiders and redeems the season.
Speech is grace-filled and salt-seasoned, answering each person wisely.
Tychicus carries news as beloved sibling and trustworthy servant.
He is sent to make things known and comfort hearts.
Onesimus returns as trustworthy and beloved sibling.
Aristarchus and Mark send greeting, with welcome commanded for Mark.
Coworkers from the circumcision comfort Paul in the reign-work.
Epaphras struggles in prayer for their maturity and full assurance.
His labor extends to Colossae, Laodicea, and Hierapolis.
Luke the beloved physician and Demas greet them.
Nympha and the assembly in her house are greeted.
Letters are shared among assemblies for mutual strengthening.
Archippus is urged to fulfill the ministry received in the Lord.
Paul signs by hand: remember my chains; grace be with you.

contemplative_note: Colossians 4 closes cosmic fullness in practical wisdom: prayer, gracious speech, dignified siblings, house-assemblies, fulfilled ministry, remembered chains, and grace.
